text: In software development, testing is crucial for building reliable applications. Unit tests check small pieces of code, like individual functions, to ensure they work correctly in isolation. Integration tests, on the other hand, examine how different parts of the system interact with each other. Many developers use test-driven development (TDD), where they write tests before the actual code. This approach helps clarify requirements and catch bugs early. Ultimately, thorough testing gives developers confidence that changes won’t break existing features, making the software more stable and easier to maintain over time.
